'''Andy Ellis''' (born {{birth date|1984|12|15|df=y}}), also known by the [[nickname]] of '''"Sniffer"''', is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Goole Vikings]] in [[RFL League 1]].

He is formerly of [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]] competition.

==Background==
Ellis was born in [[Derby]], [[Derbyshire]], England. He played rugby league for amateur club Cottingham Tigers and signing for Hull KR aged 18.<ref name="Meet Andy Ellis"/>

==Career==
In 2009 he was named Man of Steel for the Championship and also led the [[Barrow Raiders]] to the league title and to the National Rail Cup Final.<ref name="Quins acquire Barrow hooker">{{cite web|url=http://www.inthenews.co.uk/news/sport/rugby-league/quins-acquire-barrow-hooker-$1332529.htm|title=Quins acquire Barrow hooker|publisher=inthenews.co.uk|date=7 October 2009|accessdate=16 February 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20120330005525/http://www.inthenews.co.uk/news/sport/rugby-league/quins-acquire-barrow-hooker-$1332529.htm#|archive-date=30 March 2012|url-status=dead}}</ref> Ellis was also part of the Championship All Stars team in 2009 after having an incredible season with Barrow.

He has previously played for [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] and [[London Broncos#2006–2011: Harlequins Rugby League|Harlequins]]<ref name="Meet Andy Ellis">{{cite web|url=http://www.league.quins.co.uk/news/10636.php |title=Meet Andy Ellis |publisher=Harlequins RL |date=6 November 2009 |accessdate=16 February 2010 |url-status=dead |archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20091211195741/http://www.league.quins.co.uk/news/10636.php |archivedate=11 December 2009}}</ref> being named players player at Harlequins in 2011.

===Featherstone Rovers===
In September 2012, he left Wakefield and signed a two-year contract with [[Featherstone Rovers]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Featherstone swoop for Andy Ellis|url=https://loverugbyleague.com/news_10332-featherstone-swoop-for-andy-ellis.html|accessdate=15 February 2014|date=25 September 2012}}</ref> Ellis later played for Featherstone and became an integral part of the team.

===York City Knights===
On 14 June 2019, Ellis came out of retirement to fill in after injuries to York's three hookers<ref>{{cite web|website=loverugbyleague.com|date=14 June 2019|accessdate=26 December 2020|title=Andy Ellis answers York SOS|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/andy-ellis-answers-york-sos/}}</ref>

===Goole Vikings===
On 8 November 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Goole Vikings|Goole]] in the [[RFL League 1]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=8 Nov 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Goole Vikings snap up ex-Hull KR hooker|url=https://www.totalrl.com/goole-vikings-snap-up-ex-hull-kr-hooker/}}</ref>
